torch.distributed.elastic.multiprocessing.errors.ChildFailedError:
Root Cause (first observed failure):

action.hypothesis: The search has already covered multiple K values on the best mainline and recent continuous-support pivots have all underperformed, so the next informative step is to actually benchmark the newly added discrete additive family rather than return to more tuning. `product_codebook_topk` tests whether representing coarse structure with multiple retrieved codewords before a sparse residual correction can break the current fixed-TopK quality ceiling.
action.summary: Run the first proxy benchmark for `product_codebook_topk` at K=128 under the current strong shared recipe. This keeps the round architecture-only, turns the new family from code into measured signal, and directly tests the discrete additive-codebook direction before deciding whether to promote or archive it.
action.expected_win: Best case: proxy FVU lands materially below recent alternative families like `batch_topk` and `bucketed_topk`, ideally approaching the low-0.14s or better. Failure case: discrete additive codebooks also miss badly, letting us archive this branch and pivot away from codebook-style sparse search.
action.self_review: This round preserves single-variable isolation by evaluating the newly introduced family without adding optimizer or LR changes. It follows the stagnation rule by continuing the architecture pivot instead of resuming low-signal parameter sweeps.
notes_to_memory: Round 40 is the first actual proxy evaluation for `product_codebook_topk` after the family was added in round 39.
This run is meant to validate whether discrete additive codewords plus sparse residual correction produce any real quality signal, not to tune the family locally.
If this proxy is uncompetitive, codebook-style additive routing should be treated as another failed architecture branch and deprioritized.
next_hypotheses: If `product_codebook_topk` is competitive at K=128 proxy, promote it to full before any family-local tuning.
If `product_codebook_topk` shows promising quality with poor throughput, follow with a narrow `code_fix` on codeword lookup or routing rather than discarding the family.

If `product_codebook_topk` remains clearly behind, archive additive product-codebook routing and pivot to a fundamentally different direction such as input-space preprocessing or output-aware objectives rather than another sparse-allocation variant.
